Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Marcus Webb" or "cold-chain" or "Swift" (or similar) to find the email thread about the delivery issue. Expected findings: Greg Pallone's April 17 follow-up email requesting the signed shipper's declaration for temperature-controlled biological materials, and Greg's April 23 email reporting that the delivery to Canopy Health's Chicago lab receiving dock was refused because the declaration was still missing. The April 23 email mentions an $800 failed-delivery surcharge if the documentation is not provided within 48 hours, and that the containers are being held at Swift's Columbus staging facility under active cold-chain maintenance.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Patricia" or "Canopy" or "Webb" (or similar) to discover Patricia Langford's escalation. Expected findings: Patricia Langford (Senior Director, People Operations, Canopy Health) emailed Catalina Dubois on April 24 stating Marcus Webb has been at the Chicago office since April 14 without his lab equipment, referencing the Keiko Tanaka DOT placarding gap as a trust concern, and threatening to rent temporary lab equipment and deduct the cost from the MoveOps contract if delivery is not completed by Tuesday April 28.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Catalina" or "Webb" or "recovery plan" (or similar) to discover Catalina's forwarded escalation to Blessing. Expected findings: Catalina Dubois forwarded Patricia's email to Blessing on April 24, CC'ing Chloe Vance, noting she promised Patricia a service recovery plan within 24 hours and that Canopy is MoveOps's longest-standing client.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "surcharge" or "billing" or "Marcus Thorne" (or similar) to discover the billing gap email. Expected findings: Marcus Thorne emailed Chloe Vance and Catalina Dubois on April 24 flagging that INV-2026-0401 includes a $1,200 cold-chain surcharge for the Webb shipment but there is no corresponding Swift Relocations purchase order, vendor bill, or payment entry in finance.
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Marcus Webb" or "cold-chain" or "paperwork" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the delivery failure. Expected findings: Chloe Vance posted in #operations (C006) on April 24 asking Blessing for a full paperwork accounting on the Webb cold-chain shipment, connecting it to the Keiko Tanaka DOT documentation gap pattern.
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "cold-chain surcharge" or "INV-2026-0401" or "Swift" (or similar), or browse #finance (C005) using conversations_history with channel_id C005, to discover the billing gap discussion. Expected findings: Marcus Thorne posted in #finance on April 24 that the $1,200 cold-chain surcharge was invoiced to Canopy on INV-2026-0401 but there is no matching Swift purchase order, vendor bill, or payment in the books.
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Canopy" (or similar), or browse #customer-engagement (C002) using conversations_history with channel_id C002, to discover what the account team has been saying about the Canopy relationship. Expected findings: Messages from Catalina Dubois and others in #customer-engagement discussing Canopy Health account activity, including references to the Q2 relocation wave, coordinator assignments, and account management context.
OE 8: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id, table_name, field_name "Name" and value "Marcus Webb" (or similar) to find the relocation record. Expected findings: Record recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago showing Marcus Webb, Canopy Health, Detroit MI to Chicago IL, Status "In Progress", Assigned Coordinator Blessing Okafor, with special requirements describing the split-vendor arrangement (Heartland for household goods, Swift for cold-chain lab equipment: 2x Thermo CryoPlus 3800 cryogenic containers and 1x MVE SC 20/20 Dewar flask).
OE 9: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "Canopy" or "INV-2026-0401" (or similar) to verify the cold-chain surcharge billing. Expected findings: Invoice INV-2026-0401 ($9,210 total) includes a $1,200 cold-chain lab freight surcharge line item for Marcus Webb's shipment. The invoice was billed to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "Swift" (or similar) to check for a matching vendor payment. Expected findings: No vendor bill exists for Swift Relocations covering Marcus Webb's cold-chain shipment. Bills for Swift exist for other moves (e.g., Venkatesh, Kovac) but none for Webb. This confirms the billing gap Marcus Thorne flagged.
OE 11: Search CRM companies using crm_search_companies with name "Canopy Health" (or similar) to get the company record and company_id. Expected findings: Company record company_canopy showing Canopy Health, Healthcare Technology industry, Chicago IL, type "Client", with description noting it is MoveOps's longest-standing client.
OE 12: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with company_id "company_canopy" (or similar) to find all active Canopy deals and understand the full account picture. Expected findings: Multiple active deals including deal_canopy_webb_apr2026 ($3,200, inprogress, Marcus Webb Detroit to Chicago), deal_canopy_kovacs_apr2026 ($3,850, inprogress, Dr. Renata Kovacs Nashville to Toronto), deal_canopy_okonkwo_apr2026 ($2,160, inprogress, Tyler Okonkwo Philadelphia to Denver), deal_canopy_q2 ($10,000, contractsent, Q2 2026 Relocations), and crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb ($12,170, inprogress, Dr. Anand Mehta executive relocation Boston to Chicago).
OE 13: List CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_canopy" (or similar) to review the engagement history and understand what the account team has documented. Expected findings: Multiple engagements including eng_canopy_coord_assignments_001 (coordinator assignments by Chloe), eng_canopy_webb_splitvendor_001 (Blessing's split-vendor arrangement details), eng_canopy_q2_wave_crm_update_001 (Catalina's CRM update with invoice and deal details), eng_canopy_surcharge_approval_flag_001 (surcharge approval tracking), and crm_engagement_ac101931ed37 (Dr. Mehta executive relocation context).
OE 14: List Linear projects using linear_list_projects with query related to "DOT" or "compliance" (or similar) to find the compliance audit Chloe has been tracking. Expected findings: Project project_dot_compliance_audit titled "DOT Compliance Audit — Q1/Q2 2026 Hazmat Shipments" (or similar), state "started", led by Chloe Vance (moveops_chloe_vance), team Operations, triggered by PHMSA formal compliance inquiry, covering all hazmat shipments Jan-Jun 2026.
OE 15: List Linear issues using linear_list_issues with project "project_dot_compliance_audit" (or similar) to find open compliance items. Expected findings: Multiple issues including issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser, state in_progress, priority 1), issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift, state todo, priority 1), issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (Mei-Lin Zhao, done), issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (Raj Patel, done), and issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (Joaquin Reyes, done). The Tanaka issue and Swift cert subtask are still open, directly connecting to the documentation gap pattern.
OE 16: Search calendar events using calendar_search_events with query "Canopy" (or similar), or use calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with a date range covering the current week, to find the upcoming Canopy planning meeting. Expected findings: Event event_canopy_q2_planning_apr29 titled "Canopy Health Q2 Relocations Planning" scheduled for Wednesday April 29, with attendees including Blessing Okafor, Catalina Dubois, Chloe Vance, and Patricia Langford.
OE 17: Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Greg Pallone" (or similar) to get Greg's email address for the response. Expected findings: Greg Pallone, Regional Account Director at Swift Relocations, email greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, phone (614) 555-0189.
OE 18: Send an email or reply to Greg Pallone's refused-delivery email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from blessing.okafor@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, acknowledging the missed shipper's declaration, committing to provide the documentation, and requesting a revised delivery timeline for the cryogenic containers to Canopy Health's Chicago lab.
OE 19: Update the Marcus Webb Airtable relocation record (via airtable_update_records) to reflect the current delivery hold status, noting that the cold-chain shipment was refused due to missing shipper's declaration and containers are being held at Swift's Columbus facility pending documentation.
OE 20: Create a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Marcus Webb cold-chain delivery hold, assigned to the Operations team (team_operations or similar), with details about the refused delivery, missing shipper's declaration, $800 surcharge deadline, Patricia's April 28 deadline, and the need to provide documentation to Swift to release the containers.
OE 21: Add a comment to an existing DOT compliance audit issue (via linear_create_comment) on issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic or issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (or similar), connecting the Marcus Webb cold-chain documentation failure to the broader compliance pattern and noting that this is a second documentation gap involving Swift shipments.
OE 22: Post a message in Slack #operations channel (via conversations_add_message with channel_id C006 and payload) summarizing the Marcus Webb cold-chain situation for Chloe: delivery refused, containers held in Columbus, shipper's declaration needed, $800 surcharge risk, Patricia's April 28 deadline, and the steps being taken to resolve it. This directly responds to Chloe's April 24 request for a full paperwork accounting.
OE 23: Send an email (via send_email) to chloe.vance@moveops.com from blessing.okafor@moveops.com providing the status update Chloe requested, including what happened with the delivery, what documentation is missing, the recovery timeline, and the connection to the DOT compliance audit pattern.
OE 24: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) with engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), contact_ids and company_ids referencing Canopy Health, documenting the cold-chain delivery failure incident, the client escalation from Patricia Langford, the billing gap with the $1,200 surcharge, and the resolution steps being taken. This creates the paper trail the prompt requests.
OE 25: Update the Canopy Q2 planning calendar event (via calendar_edit_calendar_event with event_id "event_canopy_q2_planning_apr29" or similar) to add the cold-chain delivery situation to the meeting description or agenda, so the team is prepared for the discussion with Patricia.
OE 26: Send an email or Slack message to Catalina Dubois (via send_email to catalina.dubois@moveops.com or conversations_add_message) updating her on the cold-chain situation and resolution steps, since she is the Canopy account manager and promised Patricia a service recovery plan within 24 hours.
OE 27: Flag the billing issue to Marcus Thorne or finance (via send_email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com or conversations_add_message to #finance channel C005) confirming that no Swift purchase order or vendor bill exists for the Webb cold-chain surcharge, and providing the operational context so finance can determine next steps on the $1,200 line item on INV-2026-0401.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

vm_created_at2026-04-02T18:58:05.418Z
🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Marcus Webb" or "cold-chain" or "Swift" (or similar) to find the email thread about the delivery issue. Expected findings: Greg Pallone's April 17 follow-up email requesting the signed shipper's declaration for temperature-controlled biological materials, and Greg's April 23 email reporting that the delivery to Canopy Health's Chicago lab receiving dock was refused because the declaration was still missing. The April 23 email mentions an $800 failed-delivery surcharge if the documentation is not provided within 48 hours, and that the containers are being held at Swift's Columbus staging facility under active cold-chain maintenance.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Patricia" or "Canopy" or "Webb" (or similar) to discover Patricia Langford's escalation. Expected findings: Patricia Langford (Senior Director, People Operations, Canopy Health) emailed Catalina Dubois on April 24 stating Marcus Webb has been at the Chicago office since April 14 without his lab equipment, referencing the Keiko Tanaka DOT placarding gap as a trust concern, and threatening to rent temporary lab equipment and deduct the cost from the MoveOps contract if delivery is not completed by Tuesday April 28.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Catalina" or "Webb" or "recovery plan" (or similar) to discover Catalina's forwarded escalation to Blessing. Expected findings: Catalina Dubois forwarded Patricia's email to Blessing on April 24, CC'ing Chloe Vance, noting she promised Patricia a service recovery plan within 24 hours and that Canopy is MoveOps's longest-standing client.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "surcharge" or "billing" or "Marcus Thorne" (or similar) to discover the billing gap email. Expected findings: Marcus Thorne emailed Chloe Vance and Catalina Dubois on April 24 flagging that INV-2026-0401 includes a $1,200 cold-chain surcharge for the Webb shipment but there is no corresponding Swift Relocations purchase order, vendor bill, or payment entry in finance.
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Marcus Webb" or "cold-chain" or "paperwork" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the delivery failure. Expected findings: Chloe Vance posted in #operations (C006) on April 24 asking Blessing for a full paperwork accounting on the Webb cold-chain shipment, connecting it to the Keiko Tanaka DOT documentation gap pattern.
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "cold-chain surcharge" or "INV-2026-0401" or "Swift" (or similar), or browse #finance (C005) using conversations_history with channel_id C005, to discover the billing gap discussion. Expected findings: Marcus Thorne posted in #finance on April 24 that the $1,200 cold-chain surcharge was invoiced to Canopy on INV-2026-0401 but there is no matching Swift purchase order, vendor bill, or payment in the books.
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Canopy" (or similar), or browse #customer-engagement (C002) using conversations_history with channel_id C002, to discover what the account team has been saying about the Canopy relationship. Expected findings: Messages from Catalina Dubois and others in #customer-engagement discussing Canopy Health account activity, including references to the Q2 relocation wave, coordinator assignments, and account management context.
OE 8: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records with base_id, table_name, field_name "Name" and value "Marcus Webb" (or similar) to find the relocation record. Expected findings: Record recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago showing Marcus Webb, Canopy Health, Detroit MI to Chicago IL, Status "In Progress", Assigned Coordinator Blessing Okafor, with special requirements describing the split-vendor arrangement (Heartland for household goods, Swift for cold-chain lab equipment: 2x Thermo CryoPlus 3800 cryogenic containers and 1x MVE SC 20/20 Dewar flask).
OE 9: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "Canopy" or "INV-2026-0401" (or similar) to verify the cold-chain surcharge billing. Expected findings: Invoice INV-2026-0401 ($9,210 total) includes a $1,200 cold-chain lab freight surcharge line item for Marcus Webb's shipment. The invoice was billed to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.com.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "Swift" (or similar) to check for a matching vendor payment. Expected findings: No vendor bill exists for Swift Relocations covering Marcus Webb's cold-chain shipment. Bills for Swift exist for other moves (e.g., Venkatesh, Kovac) but none for Webb. This confirms the billing gap Marcus Thorne flagged.
OE 11: Search CRM companies using crm_search_companies with name "Canopy Health" (or similar) to get the company record and company_id. Expected findings: Company record company_canopy showing Canopy Health, Healthcare Technology industry, Chicago IL, type "Client", with description noting it is MoveOps's longest-standing client.
OE 12: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with company_id "company_canopy" (or similar) to find all active Canopy deals and understand the full account picture. Expected findings: Multiple active deals including deal_canopy_webb_apr2026 ($3,200, inprogress, Marcus Webb Detroit to Chicago), deal_canopy_kovacs_apr2026 ($3,850, inprogress, Dr. Renata Kovacs Nashville to Toronto), deal_canopy_okonkwo_apr2026 ($2,160, inprogress, Tyler Okonkwo Philadelphia to Denver), deal_canopy_q2 ($10,000, contractsent, Q2 2026 Relocations), and crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb ($12,170, inprogress, Dr. Anand Mehta executive relocation Boston to Chicago).
OE 13: List CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_canopy" (or similar) to review the engagement history and understand what the account team has documented. Expected findings: Multiple engagements including eng_canopy_coord_assignments_001 (coordinator assignments by Chloe), eng_canopy_webb_splitvendor_001 (Blessing's split-vendor arrangement details), eng_canopy_q2_wave_crm_update_001 (Catalina's CRM update with invoice and deal details), eng_canopy_surcharge_approval_flag_001 (surcharge approval tracking), and crm_engagement_ac101931ed37 (Dr. Mehta executive relocation context).
OE 14: List Linear projects using linear_list_projects with query related to "DOT" or "compliance" (or similar) to find the compliance audit Chloe has been tracking. OE 15: List Linear issues using linear_list_issues with project "project_dot_compliance_audit" (or similar) to find open compliance items. OE 16: Search calendar events using calendar_search_events with query "Canopy" (or similar), or use calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with a date range covering the current week, to find the upcoming Canopy planning meeting. Expected findings: Event event_canopy_q2_planning_apr29 titled "Canopy Health Q2 Relocations Planning" scheduled for Wednesday April 29, with attendees including Blessing Okafor, Catalina Dubois, Chloe Vance, and Patricia Langford.
OE 17: Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Greg Pallone" (or similar) to get Greg's email address for the response. Expected findings: Greg Pallone, Regional Account Director at Swift Relocations, email greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, phone (614) 555-0189.
OE 18: Send an email or reply to Greg Pallone's refused-delivery email (via send_email or reply_to_email) from blessing.okafor@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, acknowledging the missed shipper's declaration, committing to provide the documentation, and requesting a revised delivery timeline for the cryogenic containers to Canopy Health's Chicago lab.
OE 19: Update the Marcus Webb Airtable relocation record (via airtable_update_records) to reflect the current delivery hold status, noting that the cold-chain shipment was refused due to missing shipper's declaration and containers are being held at Swift's Columbus facility pending documentation.
OE 20: Create a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Marcus Webb cold-chain delivery hold, assigned to the Operations team (team_operations or similar), with details about the refused delivery, missing shipper's declaration, $800 surcharge deadline, Patricia's April 28 deadline, and the need to provide documentation to Swift to release the containers.
OE 21: Add a comment to an existing DOT compliance audit issue (via linear_create_comment) on issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic or issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (or similar), connecting the Marcus Webb cold-chain documentation failure to the broader compliance pattern and noting that this is a second documentation gap involving Swift shipments.
OE 22: Post a message in Slack #operations channel (via conversations_add_message with channel_id C006 and payload) summarizing the Marcus Webb cold-chain situation for Chloe: delivery refused, containers held in Columbus, shipper's declaration needed, $800 surcharge risk, Patricia's April 28 deadline, and the steps being taken to resolve it. This directly responds to Chloe's April 24 request for a full paperwork accounting.
OE 23: Send an email (via send_email) to chloe.vance@moveops.com from blessing.okafor@moveops.com providing the status update Chloe requested, including what happened with the delivery, what documentation is missing, the recovery timeline, and the connection to the DOT compliance audit pattern.
OE 24: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) with engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), contact_ids and company_ids referencing Canopy Health, documenting the cold-chain delivery failure incident, the client escalation from Patricia Langford, the billing gap with the $1,200 surcharge, and the resolution steps being taken. This creates the paper trail the prompt requests.
OE 25: Update the Canopy Q2 planning calendar event (via calendar_edit_calendar_event with event_id "event_canopy_q2_planning_apr29" or similar) to add the cold-chain delivery situation to the meeting description or agenda, so the team is prepared for the discussion with Patricia.
OE 26: Send an email or Slack message to Catalina Dubois (via send_email to catalina.dubois@moveops.com or conversations_add_message) updating her on the cold-chain situation and resolution steps, since she is the Canopy account manager and promised Patricia a service recovery plan within 24 hours.
OE 27: Flag the billing issue to Marcus Thorne or finance (via send_email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com or conversations_add_message to #finance channel C005) confirming that no Swift purchase order or vendor bill exists for the Webb cold-chain surcharge, and providing the operational context so finance can determine next steps on the $1,200 line item on INV-2026-0401.

✓Run #4The quickbooks_search_invoices call used criteria {'DocNumber': 'INV-2026-0401'} (the specific invoice number). The quickbooks_search_bills call used fetchAll to look for any Swift bills. These queries are sufficient to investigate the billing concern with relevant identifiers.
